title: Plugin-registered Janet tools bypass the permission engine
description: JanetLoopTool::execute (plugin/extension.rs:377-463; builder/loop_tools.rs:522-538) runs the Janet handler with NO enforce()/permission check, unlike every built-in tool. Janet has full file/network/shell stdlib (only os/exit, proc-kill, sigaction neutered). An LLM calling a plugin-registered tool performs arbitrary side effects without hitting allow/deny/ask. Tool-name shadowing is blocked and before/after hooks fire, but neither enforces user permission. Fix: thread a PermCheck into JanetLoopTool::from_meta and gate execute like built-ins.
close_reason: Plugin tools now route through the permission chokepoint. Added Operation::Plugin (high-risk: not builtin-allowed, not Accept-coerced, like MCP/bash). JanetLoopTool gates execute() via a deny_tools probe (concrete name + plugin_tool umbrella) + enforce(plugin_tool, Scope::Raw(name)) before any Janet dispatch. Default Ask in standard mode, Allow under yolo, Deny via deny_tools/rules. Tests: 3 in extension.rs (deny/ask-noninteractive/yolo-runs), 2 in checker_tests (accept-not-coerced, yolo-allows/standard-asks), 1 mapping assertion.

title: HIGH: tool output not scrubbed for secrets before LLM/session storage
description: Secret redaction (sandbox.rs is_sensitive_env_name/value) is applied only to subprocess env (input). Tool RESULTS flow verbatim to the LLM (via cap_oversized_tool_results) and to session storage. cat .env / echo $API_KEY / env leak. Fix: a single redact_secrets() boundary on tool output reusing the existing detector. sandbox.rs:121,181; ui/events.rs:317 (ANSI-only).

title: HIGH: /allow remove <n> doesn't revoke engine grant
description: remove_session_allowlist_at only mutates the display list; the engine allowlist (runtime source of truth read by SessionAllowlistPolicy) keeps the grant. Revoked perms stay active. Must remove engine entry by matched (op, original), not index.
close_reason: Fixed + merged in PR #204 (TDD, 4 new tests); 2104 tests pass at -D warnings

title: Perm review F1: bash arg-side path checks for file-mutating commands
description:
SECURITY GAP found in opencode-vs-dirge review. Today (post-M3, fbcc09b) dirge's bash permission flow extracts ONLY redirect targets (>, >>, &>, etc.) via extract_redirect_targets and routes them through write rules. Arguments of file-mutating commands (rm, cp, mv, chmod, chown, ln, mkdir, rmdir, touch, tee, dd) are NOT extracted — they go only through the bash command-pattern rules. 

Concrete bypass: a user who configures bash rules permissively (e.g., 'rm *: allow' for convenience) silently allows 'rm /etc/passwd' even though write rules deny /etc/**. Opencode (shell.ts:374-410) walks the 'command' AST nodes, identifies file-mutating heads, and routes each positional path arg through the external_directory / write permission.

Port: extend src/semantic/adapters/bash.rs with an extract_mutation_paths(command) function that walks the tree-sitter 'command' nodes; for each command whose head matches the list above, extract positional args that look like paths (skip -flags / --long-flags) and emit them. In src/agent/tools/bash.rs check_bash_segments, after the existing redirect-target loop, walk extracted mutation paths and route through enforce(tool='write', Scope::PathResolve(path)).

title: Perm M3: port maki's tree-sitter bash analyzer (close git&&rm bypass)
description:
SECURITY: 'git diff && rm -rf /' currently allowed because dirge's bash redirect-target check (src/agent/tools/bash.rs:350) routes through bash rules with the file path as input, which has no path-style match → falls to default Allow. Pre-existing pre-fix-for-7403792.

Solution: port maki's tree-sitter bash analyzer verbatim from /Users/yogthos/src/maki/maki-agent/src/permissions.rs:33-43 (parser thread_local), 394-439 (collect_commands walker), 441-475 (analyze_bash + complexity gates). The walker splits compounds via 'pipeline'/'list' AST traversal and extracts every 'command' / 'redirected_statement' / 'subshell' / etc node; each segment then goes through the permission chokepoint independently.

Behavior at completion:
- 'git diff && rm -rf /' → enforce('bash', 'git diff') + enforce('bash', 'rm -rf /') — second check fires
- Subshells / command substitution mark whole command 'complex' → forces prompt (conservative)
- Pipes split into separate segments
- Quoted operators correctly NOT split (AST respects quoting)

Depends on: dirge-{M1}

Maki license is GPL-compatible — verify before copying. Add 'Ported from maki-agent/src/permissions.rs' attribution comment.

title: SQLite memory store: backend swap with behavior parity
description: Replace MEMORY.md/PITFALLS.md markdown files + .meta.json/.usage.json sidecars with a memories table in the per-project session DB (state.db). Migration v7: memories table (id, target, kind, content, status, tier, confidence, salience, created_at, updated_at, last_used_at, use_count, superseded_by) + memories_fts with sync triggers. New SqliteMemoryStore implementing MemoryProvider. One-time importer from existing markdown files + sidecars; originals renamed *.imported. Threat scanning preserved at write and prompt-render. Rewire memory curator scan_entries and cross-session extractor existing_memory_lowercased onto the store API. Kills the .meta.json lost-update race, file locks, drift detection. Tests: parity suite, import round-trip, concurrent writes.

title: sandbox-microvm feature does not compile on macOS (copy_file_range + libkrun link)
description: v0.4.0: building with --features sandbox-microvm on macOS fails. (1) libc::copy_file_range in rootfs.rs is Linux-only -> lib+main bin fail to compile. (2) dirge-microvm-runner links libkrun symbols which don't exist on macOS (libkrun is Linux/KVM-only). Feature was never gated to Linux. Fix: cfg(target_os=linux)-gate the reflink path (fallback to std::fs::copy) and the runner's libkrun body (stub main on non-Linux).
close_reason: Fixed in c688093, released in v0.4.1. cfg(target_os=linux)-gated copy_file_range (std::fs::copy fallback) and the runner's libkrun body (Linux-only stub main); gated the two reflink syscall tests. --features sandbox-microvm + --all-features now build on macOS; verified locally. Runtime still requires Linux + KVM.

title: Queued interjection cannot be dropped; on-screen hint says Alt+X but it is unimplemented
description: ui/mod.rs:1679 prints '(queued; ... Alt+X drops, Ctrl+C cancels)' but there is no Alt+X handler anywhere and no key handler that clears interjection_queue short of a full Ctrl+C/Esc cancel. Ctrl+X is bound to CloseChat (keymap.rs:88-92), unrelated. So the documented Ctrl+X/Alt+X queue-drop does not exist. Fix: implement a queue-drop key (Alt+X) or correct the hint + docs.
close_reason: KeyAction::DropQueue bound to Alt+X (Ctrl+X stays close_chat); event-loop handler drains interjection_queue + reports count without cancelling the run. Docs reconciled to Alt+X. Test: keymap::alt_x_drops_queue_ctrl_x_closes_chat.

title: Model-family steering keys off launch-time model, not the active (swapped) model
description: builder/agent_inner.rs:206 derives the steering family from cli.resolve_provider/resolve_model (cli.rs:229-258), which read only CLI flags + config Default role, never session.model or the swapped model. After any /model or /agent model swap: switching TO a DeepSeek-chat model omits the DeepSeek guidance fragment (false negative); switching AWAY still appends it (false positive). Fix: feed the actual active model name + resolved provider into resolve_family.
close_reason: build_agent_inner now takes the active provider+model (parent_model.name()) and feeds resolve_family with them instead of cli.resolve_model. Steering tracks /model and /agent swaps. Test: steering_fragment_tracks_active_model_not_cli.

title: Post-merge cwd restore fires unconditionally even when the merge failed
description: done.rs:593-642: wt_return_path.take() fires on the FIRST Done after a /wt-merge run regardless of merge success. If the merge agent stopped early (conflict/error/mid-task Done), cwd, session.working_dir, permission root, and agent are re-anchored to main while the worktree may still hold un-merged changes, and 'merged and returned to main repo' prints anyway. Fix: gate the return on a verified-clean merge signal. Related to the /wt-merge LLM-merge issue.
close_reason: git_worktree::merge_worktree does the merge programmatically: refuses on dirty worktree/main, git merge --no-ff, git merge --abort on conflict (repo untouched), never pushes/deletes. DEFER_WT_MERGE handler calls it directly (no LLM) and restores cwd + removes worktree only on clean merge. Unconditional post-Done restore + wt_return_path/WorktreeBits removed. Tests: clean/conflict-abort/dirty-refuse.

title: /wt-merge delegates the whole merge to an unconstrained LLM prompt (data-loss risk)
description: cmd_worktree.rs:94-135 + ui/mod.rs:1531-1558: cmd_wt_merge only emits a DEFER_WT_MERGE sentinel; the handler builds a natural-language prompt ('merge branch X into Y, push, delete the worktree') and hands it to a normal agent run. No programmatic git merge, no conflict detection, no abort-on-conflict, and the model is told to delete the worktree in the same instruction. Conflict or push failure can lose un-merged work or leave a half-merged repo. Fix: perform the merge programmatically with conflict detection; never delete the worktree unless merge+push verified clean.
close_reason: git_worktree::merge_worktree does the merge programmatically: refuses on dirty worktree/main, git merge --no-ff, git merge --abort on conflict (repo untouched), never pushes/deletes. DEFER_WT_MERGE handler calls it directly (no LLM) and restores cwd + removes worktree only on clean merge. Unconditional post-Done restore + wt_return_path/WorktreeBits removed. Tests: clean/conflict-abort/dirty-refuse.

title: Automatic in-loop LLM compaction is dead in production (summarize_fn never set)
description: LoopSpawnConfig.summarize_fn is never assigned outside test mocks (provider/spawn.rs:147-194,358). So proactive folds at 75/78/80/90% (run.rs:410) degrade to prune_tool_outputs only. That prune itself no-ops on production tool results: compression.rs:419 reads content as_str() but production tool_result content is a block array (message.rs:289). Real LLM summarization only happens reactively via handle_compress/ContextOverflow. Docs (agent-loop.md/features.md) claim automatic structured-summary compaction at the turn boundary. Confirmed by session + provider reviews. Fix: wire cfg.summarize_fn from AnyClient::compress_messages OR correct the docs; also make prune_tool_outputs handle block arrays. NOTE: before wiring, fix tool-pair boundary snapping in compute_compress_window/apply_summary (see separate issue) to avoid orphaned tool_use/tool_result pairs (400s).
close_reason: SummarizeFn built at build_agent from the main model, stored on AnyAgent (with_summarizer), forwarded in spawn_runner. Proactive folds now run LLM summarization. Unblocked by 89fm + u5ka. Tests: with_summarizer_stashes + run_tests compaction suite.

title: /agent and /prompt share one deny_tools slot and clobber each other
description: cmd_model.rs:303 (/prompt) and :431 (/agent) both write current_prompt_deny_tools; set_prompt_deny_tools (permission/checker.rs:248) REPLACES rather than merges. So /prompt review (denies edit/write/bash) then /agent <name> wipes the deny-list, re-enabling edit/write/bash while the agent still believes it is in plan/review mode. Security-boundary regression, no warning. Confirmed independently by permission + provider reviews. Fix: merge deny-lists, or refuse a profile that weakens an active read-only prompt.
notes: USER DIRECTIVE: fix as a UNIFIED mechanism, not a point patch. The conflict surface (deny_tools single-slot clobber dirge-x7c8, model-not-reverted dirge-anhw, reviewer-fork bash deniable) all stem from profile/prompt/permission composition writing to shared single-slot state and overwriting instead of merging/layering. Design one composition layer (e.g. a layered stack: base prompt -> active /prompt -> active /agent profile, each contributing prompt+model+deny/allow, with explicit revert) covering all three. Treat dirge-x7c8 + dirge-anhw together.
close_reason: Unified composition layer (ContextFiles::recompute_composition): /prompt and /agent are independent layers folded into the effective fields. Denies UNION (no clobber); /agent off restores prompt layer + pre-agent model (model_before_agent). All set-sites routed through layer setters. 6 composition unit tests.

title: UI loop hangs / blocks on many actions
description: The TUI frequently becomes blocked/unresponsive during various actions. Review how the UI event loop works (ui/mod.rs run loop, blocking slash handlers, render/draw calls, channel waits, sync handler work on the UI thread) and identify what blocks the loop. Likely culprits: synchronous/blocking work performed inline on the UI thread (slash handlers, compaction, network, subprocess), awaits that park the event loop, or lock contention. Diagnose the blocking points and fix so the UI stays responsive.
notes: Primary fix (status-line git branch caching) merged in #374. REMAINING follow-up: /plan runs its explore/plan forks inline on the UI loop (cmd_plan.rs run_phases) — parks the event loop for the LLM-call duration. Fix is to spawn the phase runners + monitor via channel. Larger change; left open.
close_reason: Merged in #379 — /plan forks now run off the event loop; loop stays responsive

title: Finish rig→loop tool phase-out: delete duplicated tool construction
description: loop_tools.rs:91 comment admits tool construction is 'temporarily duplicated' between build_agent_inner (legacy rig path, retained only for the rig Agent preamble) and build_loop_tools (live dispatch). Comment states the rig Agent's tools are 'no longer invoked after phase 4.5h-6'. Adding any tool currently requires editing both. Confirm the rig dispatch path is truly dead, then remove the duplicate construction so tools are defined once. MED-HIGH value.

title: Consolidate message→JSON serialization (3 copies)
description: serialize_assistant (agent_loop/stream.rs:354) and loop_message_to_value (agent_loop/run.rs:1485, agent_loop/integration.rs:698 + nested assistant_to_value :700) all emit the identical {role,content,stopReason,errorMessage} JSON shape. Three private copies that must stay in lockstep. Extract one pub fn serialize_message(&LoopMessage)->Value into agent_loop/message.rs and route all sites through it. ~80 LOC removed, single source of truth. Verified via grep. No behavior change — pure de-dup.
close_reason: Merged in #362 — single serialize_message source of truth in message.rs

title: Round B: merge sanitize_output into strip_escapes + require_and_resolve tool preamble
description: #1 sanitize_output and strip_escapes are byte-identical DoS-capped ANSI state machines (45 call sites); ansi.rs:94-98 already flags the merge as intended-but-unfinished. Make sanitize_output = strip_escapes(strip_orphan_mouse_reports(s), KEEP_BOTH); delete the ~80-LOC duplicate. Security-relevant (gates untrusted LLM/bash output). #3 tools::require_and_resolve centralizing require_absolute_path + check_perm_path_resolve (Audit-H12 symlink-swap invariant) across 8 tools.

title: Critic reads stale compaction summary as live requirements
description: The in-loop critic (critic_provider tier 3) is fed the agent's merged system prompt as its 'rules to judge against'. That merged prompt is preamble + history, and history concatenates all System messages — including the context-compaction summary ([CONTEXT COMPACTION — REFERENCE ONLY]). The summary's '## Active Task' describes work that was current at compaction time but has since been completed, so the critic blocks finalization on already-done/superseded work (observed: it demanded a 'Phase 3 / Janet loader' that didn't exist in the session). Fix: strip the compaction summary from the critic's rules + harden the preamble to discount REFERENCE-ONLY blocks.
close_reason: stale compaction summary stripped from critic rules (#346)

title: P1: Exploration tool-frugality rules in plan/explore prompts
description: Add explicit frugality rules to dirge's plan/explore prompt path: minimize tool calls, ban orientation reads, never call same tool on same file twice, force a 2-3 sentence summary instead of dumping files. PORT vix prompts/plan_workflow/explore.md:16-35.

title: P1: Todo-completion nudge (block premature end_turn with pending todos)
description: On end_turn, if pending/in_progress todos remain, inject up to 3 nudges to finish or clear the list before stopping. PORT vix internal/daemon/session.go:1551-1558 + hasPendingTodos (session_todo.go).

title: P1: Mandatory reason/intent fields on read/grep/glob/find/lsp + bash anti-misuse fields
description: Add a required 'reason' string (what file, expected info, goal) to read/grep/glob/find_files/lsp tool schemas, and bash reason_to_use_instead_of_{read,edit,glob} fields. Surface in tool-call UI. PORT vix tool_schemas.go:117-122 (reason) + :210-221 (bash justification).

title: P1: Hard read-before-edit gate
description: Block edit/apply_patch unless the target file was read this session; mark a path read on successful read/edit/write so chained edits skip re-reads. Error tells the model to read first. PORT vix internal/daemon/session_read_gate.go:45-87 (readTrackingTools + gate). dirge today only has a prompt hint (exemplars.rs, tool_input_repair/hints.rs).

title: Port battle-tested vix agentic-loop features into dirge (phased)
description: Port proven mechanisms from the vix coding agent (~/src/vix) into dirge's agent loop to improve coding-model capability. Port vix's logic faithfully rather than reinventing. Phase 1 = quick wins; Phase 2 = efficiency; Phase 3 = meatier workflow/verification changes. See review in session.
close_reason: All phases landed: P1 quick wins, P2 efficiency (minify, read-gate, watchdog), P3 phased plan workflow (explore→plan→implement→reviewer-loop via /plan). 16/16 sub-tasks merged to main.

title: DAP hardening: fix review findings from #318 (UB, hangs, panics, tests)
description: Follow-up to #318. Fix: C1 mem::zeroed Sender UB (janet_bindings); H1 missing DAP write timeout (client); H2 truncate UTF-8 panic on adapter output (session); M1 process-guard drop order + pgid>1 guard (client); M2 Janet dap_send_and_wait ignores shutdown; M3 event handler under lock (client); M4 Runtime::new test compile break (debug.rs); M5 UI snapshot starvation; plus framing tests, perm-deny test, dap_context or→and, dap_profiler s/ms, remove LOOP_PLAN.md, graceful disconnect.

title: Session persistence: resumed sessions self-conflict after first save, stop persisting
description: save_session(&Session) compares on-disk mtime to session.loaded_mtime to detect concurrent writers, but never refreshes loaded_mtime after its own write (signature is immutable). For a RESUMED session (loaded_mtime=Some), the first save advances the file mtime beyond the recorded loaded_mtime; every subsequent save then sees disk_mtime > loaded_mtime, falsely detects a concurrent instance, diverts the write to <id>.conflict-<ts>.json, and returns Err. Net: the real session file stops updating after the first save and conflict files pile up — 'session doesn't get saved consistently'. Fresh sessions (loaded_mtime=None) are unaffected. Fix: save_session(&mut Session) refreshes loaded_mtime to the just-written file's mtime after the atomic write.

title: Plugin can crash the whole tool via Janet os/exit (bypasses hook try-wrapping)
description: Plugins run in init_with_default_env() — the full Janet stdlib, including os/exit which calls C exit() and terminates the entire dirge process. This bypasses the (try ...) that wraps every hook/command/tool dispatch (mod.rs:275,671) and the FFI catch_unwind guards, so a buggy or hostile plugin can take down all of dirge ('the plugin asked dirge to quit'). Fix: neuter os/exit (and process-control escape hatches) in the shared plugin env so they raise a CATCHABLE Janet error instead of terminating the host; the existing dispatch try-wrapping then surfaces it as a '[plugin] ... errored' notification and dirge survives.
close_reason: Fixed in #284: HARNESS_SANDBOX neuters os/exit / os/proc-kill / os/sigaction in the shared plugin env to raise catchable errors, so a plugin can no longer terminate the host. Confirmed sole plugin-reachable quit path. Tests + full plugin suite green.

title: edit replace_all panics/corrupts on overlapping fuzzy-match ranges
description: When exact match fails and a fuzzy fallback fires with replace_all:true, the matchers can return overlapping byte ranges; the reverse-splice loop then corrupts/panics. Fix: sort ranges by start and drop overlaps before splicing (ideally in the matchers).
close_reason: Fixed in round 3 stability batch (TDD): LSP SpawnSlotGuard, edit keep_disjoint_ranges, memory superset-drift, per-attempt lock staleness. Full suite green.

title: LSP cancelled spawn leaks the spawning slot, disabling the server for the session
description: LspManager::get_or_spawn inserts an Arc<Notify> into state.spawning then awaits do_spawn; cleanup runs only after the await. If the future is dropped (Ctrl-C abort during a cold rust-analyzer/jdtls spawn) the slot is orphaned and that server is permanently unspawnable for the session. Fix: Drop guard that removes the slot + notify_waiters on drop.
close_reason: Fixed in round 3 stability batch (TDD): LSP SpawnSlotGuard, edit keep_disjoint_ranges, memory superset-drift, per-attempt lock staleness. Full suite green.

title: cd into another dir bypasses external-write gating for relative redirect/mutation targets
description: bash compound like `cd /etc && echo x > passwd` writes outside the project with no prompt: cd is auto-allowed, the relative redirect target is classified against the static project cwd (in_cwd:true, builtin-allowed), but bash cd-s first so the write lands at /etc/passwd. check_bash_segments has no cd awareness. Fix: track per-segment cwd from leading cd/pushd and anchor relative redirect/mutation targets there, or treat them as external/Ask when a cd-outside-project precedes them.
close_reason: relative redirect/mutation targets now resolved against the cd-folded effective cwd; out-of-project writes gate. Merged.

title: UTF-8 byte-slice truncation panics (7 sites)
description: Multiple &s[..N] byte slices on model/user/path content panic on multibyte UTF-8 at the cut. Sites: tool_input_repair.rs:1100 (format_structured_error, crashes the agent run), tui/panels.rs:387 (crashes TUI), session_search.rs:445 truncate_content, tools.rs:293/349 repair telemetry, memory_store.rs:576 truncate_for_error, skills/manager.rs:277 truncate, cmd_session.rs &id[..8] x4. Fix: a char-boundary-safe truncate helper applied to all sites + tests with CJK/emoji.
close_reason: char-safe crate::text::head/tail applied to all 7 byte-slice sites. Merged.

title: Loop: orphaned tool_call_id (partial storm suppression / interrupted batch) 400s the next request
description: When the storm breaker suppresses SOME tool calls in a multi-call assistant message (partial), or a batch is cancelled/interrupted mid-way, fewer tool-result messages are appended than there were tool_calls. The assistant message keeps all tool_calls, so the next provider request has an unanswered tool_call_id and 400s ('must be followed by tool messages responding to each tool_call_id'). Recoverable harness bug. Fix: backfill a synthetic error tool-result for every unanswered id so history stays well-formed and the model sees the gap.

title: Permission: allow-always never matches multi-line bash commands (regex not DOTALL)
description: Root cause of persistent re-prompts. Command-style permission patterns (Pattern::new_command, path_style=false) compile to a regex WITHOUT the DOTALL flag, so '.*' cannot span newlines. A session grant like 'npx *' -> '^npx(?: .*)?$' fails to match 'npx tsx -e "import x\nimport y"' (multi-line -e script), so the command re-prompts forever even after the user picks allow-always. Models very commonly emit multi-line -e/-c scripts and heredoc-ish args, so this breaks allow-always broadly. Fix: build command-style regexes with RegexBuilder::dot_matches_new_line(true); keep path patterns line-sensitive. Add a deterministic test corpus of realistic compound/multi-line commands verifying the suggest->allow->recheck invariant.
close_reason: 3 root causes fixed (DOTALL command regex, bare-command ** optional args, benign-builtin default-allow alignment) + npx/node/python/python3 removed from defaults (arbitrary code) + deterministic gating corpus. Merged in PR #271.

title: Background-injected MCP tools invisible under dynamic_tool_search
description: Regression from PR #266 (background MCP loading). When dynamic_tool_search is enabled, build_loop_tools snapshots the ToolSearchTool registry + an empty loaded-set at BUILD time, before MCP connects. extend_loop_tools appends MCP tools to loop_tools but updates neither, so filter_tool_defs (rig_stream_factory.rs) filters them out of every request AND tool_search can't discover them — the model can neither see nor call background-loaded MCP tools. Fix: extend_loop_tools must also mark injected tool names into the shared tool_def_filter loaded-set so they pass the request filter. dynamic_tool_search is opt-in so default users are unaffected.
close_reason: extend_loop_tools now marks injected MCP tool names into the tool_def_filter loaded-set so they pass the request filter under dynamic_tool_search. Merged in PR #267.

title: harness/lsp: startup deadlock if plugin calls harness/lsp at load time
description: LSP responder spawns after plugin load; a top-level (harness/lsp) call at load blocks the worker on reply_rx forever (no drainer yet), and main can't reach the spawn. Fix: spawn responder before plugin-load loop.

title: Questionnaire custom answer: text runs off to the side instead of soft-wrapping
description: When typing a custom answer in the question UI, text scrolls off horizontally and disappears. Need soft-wrap onto new lines and to keep the cursor/end-of-text always visible.
close_reason: Merged in PR #216: questionnaire custom answer soft-wraps via wrap_editor

title: Input textbox: doesn't scroll past max height; typed text vanishes; no up/down arrow nav
description: The TUI compose input box stops expanding at a max height and further typed lines aren't rendered (text disappears). Need: vertical scroll so the newest lines stay visible, and Up/Down arrow keys to move the cursor across the wrapped/entered lines.
notes: Scroll half fixed in PR (input-scroll-and-nav). Arrow up/down soft-wrap nav split to a follow-up issue (needs display/raw cursor mapping).

title: LLM connect/send errors ('error sending request') not retried
description: classify_error returns Other for reqwest connect/send failures like 'Http client error: error sending request for url (...)', so the retrying_stream_fn wrapper does not retry them. Add 'error sending request' (and related connect-failure shapes) to the Network branch so transient connection failures get the standard backoff retry.

title: MED-HIGH: retry loop scaffolding re-implemented 3x; btw/summarize ignore cancellation
description: recovery.rs classification is shared, but the attempt/loop/backoff-sleep driver is hand-copied 3x. Only agent_loop/retry.rs honors AbortSignal during backoff and emits StreamEvent::Retry; provider/mod.rs:593 (btw_query) and provider/summarize.rs:141 sleep uninterruptibly and retry silently. Fix: policy.run_with_retry(fn, signal) helper owning the cancellable loop + retry callback.
notes: Duplication half fixed + merged in PR #209 (run_with_retry helper). RESIDUAL: cancellation-during-backoff needs an AbortSignal threaded through btw_query/summarize call sites — still open.
close_reason: Duplication fixed in PR #209 (run_with_retry). Cancellation-during-backoff residual is narrow: only /btw is affected (the task tool already drop-cancels btw_query via select! racing), and threading an AbortSignal through the signal-less call sites isn't worth the API churn for that one path. Triage-closed.

title: MED-HIGH: lsp anchors relative paths to self.cwd before perm check
description: lsp.rs joins relative paths to self.cwd before check_perm_path_resolve, while read/write/edit/apply_patch reject relative and let the engine resolve against working_dir. self.cwd can drift from engine working_dir, so the path checked != path used. Fix: route through shared resolve_absolute / pass raw like other FS tools. lsp.rs:226 vs read.rs:184/write.rs:94/edit.rs:137.
close_reason: Fixed + merged in PR #208: lsp rejects relative paths via shared guard; cwd anchor removed

title: HIGH: UI re-hardcodes divergent path-tool list vs engine is_path_tool_name
description: UI hardcodes a smaller path-tool set (read|write|edit|list_dir) in 3 files; the engine canonical is_path_tool_name has ~14 incl semantic tools. grep/glob/apply_patch/find_files/lsp/repo_overview/semantic mislabeled in UI. Fix: UI calls engine::is_path_tool_name. tool_display.rs:35, permission_ui.rs:29, ui/mod.rs:2555 vs engine/classify.rs:36. Part of chokepoint-A.
close_reason: Fixed + merged in PR #206: storm classifiers derive from tool_operation; permission_ui path arms deduped

title: HIGH: storm-breaker uses hardcoded mutating/exempt tool lists vs metadata
description: Doom-loop storm-breaker partitions tools via its own default_mutating/default_exempt name lists (comment admits 'kept in sync with BUILTIN_TOOL_NAMES'). A new mutating tool gets execution_mode=Sequential (dispatches right) but is absent from default_mutating, so the loop guard goes blind to it. Fix: consult tool.execution_mode()/metadata. storm.rs:216,230 vs builder.rs:743,1581,1625. Part of chokepoint-A (tool metadata registry).

A model emitting DSML invoke blocks in regular text content (the common R1-in-content case) is silently missed. Extend the filter_map to include Text blocks. description: Reasonix runs truncation BEFORE storm filter (repair/index.ts:88-109 then 113-121). Dirge runs truncation inside validate_and_repair at dispatch time — AFTER storm filter. Result: two calls whose truncated args repair to the same form survive storm and dispatch identically. Move repair_truncated_json invocation to run.rs between scavenge merge and storm.filter_calls; remove the pre-pass from validate_and_repair. title: Pillar 2 / Phase 1: truncation brace-closer in tool_input_repair

Add ratatui dep, define a Layout struct that computes named Rects (chat, left_panel, right_panel, top_frame, chat_bot_frame, bottom_strip, avatar_box, input_box, right_margin, status) from (cols, rows, input_rows, overlay_active). title: Empty Read chambers re-manifesting on parallel reads
description:
User reports: agent does 7 parallel Read calls. First 6 chambers show TOP + BOTTOM only (no content). LAST chamber has full content. Same symptom shape as dirge-jzj (closed in commit 70e7290) but appears to be regressing or has a separate cause.

Reproduction: parent agent doing 'Let me look at the remaining files' while 4 background subagents are running (at the cap). Reads in parent fire in parallel (agent_loop default is Parallel). ToolResults arrive out-of-order; fresh-chamber preamble at ui/mod.rs:2597 should paint a new chamber + content for each non-current id but isn't.

Possible regressions:
- Phase D-E (subagent_chat_rx handler) added new mpsc::select arm. If event ordering interacts badly with parallel ToolResult handling, chambers could be racing.
- write_line_to_chat for inactive chats pushes to slot.buffer + bumps slot.lines. Could affect content_row() computation if called between a parent's chamber-open and content-paint? But the active chat is always 0; slot writes go to non-active. Shouldn't matter.
- Auto-resume gate (dirge-9xo) is gated on !is_running — shouldn't fire during parent tool execution.

Needs: live RUST_LOG trace of LoopEvent::ToolExecutionStart / End for one such sequence to see WHICH ids land in WHICH state at the preamble check. Static analysis maxed out.
notes: Diagnostic instrumentation landed in commit 311f208. Reproduce with RUST_LOG=dirge::ui::chamber=trace; the per-event trace stream will show whether the body is missing on arrival (tool-side bug) or being dropped by the chamber routing (UI-side bug).
close_reason: Cannot reproduce after extensive investigation. 15 isolated tests + 4 parallel layer audits (bridge, tokio::select!, LoopTool dispatch, renderer) all clear. The dirge-jzj fix logic is intact in the current code path (verified via git blame). Diagnostic tracing remains in place under 'dirge::ui::chamber=trace' if the symptom ever reappears in production. See ba35f6a + this commit for the full investigation trail.

title: Subagent: main agent doesn't auto-resume when background subagent completes
description:
Currently when a background subagent (task tool with background=true) finishes, BackgroundStore::notify queues a system-reminder. The parent agent only sees it on the NEXT user turn via prepend_pending_notifications. So the parent sits idle until the user prompts.

Opencode's approach (packages/opencode/src/tool/task.ts:215-240, resumeWhenIdle + continueIfIdle): when a background subagent completes, opencode injects a synthetic user message and starts a new turn on the parent — auto-resuming the loop.

Fix for dirge: in the UI loop's subagent_chat_rx handler (Complete/Failed events), if the parent is currently idle, automatically spawn a new turn with the pending-notification text. The mechanism is already there (BackgroundStore has notifications, prepend_pending_notifications composes the prompt) — just trigger it instead of waiting for user input.

title: UI: allow safe slash commands while agent is running
description:
Today every keypress with an agent active hits 'agent is busy — wait, interrupt (Ctrl+C), or use /quit' (src/ui/mod.rs:2013). But read-only commands (/tasks, /help, /sessions list, /tree show) don't actually need the agent to be idle. They just inspect state. Maki and opencode let inspection commands run during agent activity.

Fix: maintain an allowlist of safe-during-agent commands. The dispatch path checks the allowlist BEFORE the busy gate — anything in the list runs immediately; everything else still gates on is_running.

Allowlist (read-only / inspection):
- /tasks (lists chat windows)
- /help
- /sessions (no-arg: list)
- /tree (no-arg: show)
- /memory list
- /skill list
- /prompt (no-arg: show active)
- /model (no-arg: show active)

Commands that MUTATE state (/clear, /compress, /cd, /prompt set, /model switch) stay gated.

title: UI: ! / !! bash escape from input prompt (port from maki)
description:
Typing '!<cmd>' at the input prompt should execute the shell command and feed the output back to the agent. '!!<cmd>' executes WITHOUT telling the agent (silent — purely for the user's local terminal use).

Port verbatim: maki-ui/src/app/shell.rs:27-50 parse_shell_prefix() — self-contained, no ratatui coupling. Returns ShellPrefix { prefix_len, command, visible }.

Integration: in dirge's submission path (src/ui/mod.rs, where Enter triggers send-to-agent), detect '!' / '!!' prefix BEFORE forwarding to agent. Spawn the shell via tokio::process; on completion: if visible=true, inject as a user-visible message that flows to the agent; if visible=false, print to scrollback and skip the agent.
close_reason: Already implemented in src/shell.rs:1+ (ShellPrefix::Visible/Invisible) and wired at src/ui/mod.rs:1706/1737. Initial audit missed it because the module lives at src/shell.rs not src/ui/shell.rs.

title: UI: fuzzy search Ctrl-F via nucleo_matcher (port from maki)
description:
Current Ctrl-F search (src/ui/mod.rs:1369-1410, update_search at :4937) uses substring match via line.to_lowercase().contains(&query_lower). Maki uses nucleo_matcher for fuzzy matching ranked by score.

Port: copy from maki-ui/src/components/search_modal.rs:
- lines 8-9 (nucleo imports)
- lines 24-29 (SearchMatch struct)
- lines 157-185 (update_matches: nucleo::Atom::indices, sort by score descending)
- lines 305-351 (match-building from buffer lines)

Keep dirge's existing search overlay rendering (draw_search_bar) and key handling. Just swap the matching algorithm. Add nucleo-matcher to Cargo.toml.

title: UI: raw-mode bare LF in renderer.write produces staircase on reasoning stream
description:
USER REPORT: reasoning text (DarkMagenta) renders with each word on a new line, progressively indented — classic raw-mode \n-without-\r staircase pattern. Pre-existing bug (predates today's work).

The renderer has two writeln!(stdout, ...) call sites that emit bare LF:
- src/ui/renderer.rs:741 — write_line: writeln!(stdout, "{}", chunk) — chunk + \n
- src/ui/renderer.rs:809 — write (streaming inline): bare writeln!(stdout) — \n only

In raw mode (enable_raw_mode disables OPOST/ONLCR), \n is JUST LF: cursor moves down one row but column stays. Each subsequent call DOES MoveTo before writing, which should reset the col — yet user reports staircase. Likely cause: a buffering / order-of-ops interaction where the MoveTo escape sequence lands AFTER the LF reaches the terminal, or somewhere the MoveTo is skipped on a corner case.

Defensive fix: replace both writeln! sites with explicit write!(stdout, "\r\n") (or chunk + \r\n). Belt-and-suspenders: even if MoveTo handles it correctly normally, the explicit CR resets col immediately so any later code that bypasses MoveTo also stays sane.

User context: iTerm2, no tmux. Reasoning text only — content tokens render fine (they use replace_from + render_viewport which paints per-row with explicit MoveTo).

title: Perm review F2: alias write/apply_patch to edit permission
description:
HIGH-correctness gap found in opencode-vs-dirge review. Opencode (permission/index.ts:291-301) defines EDIT_TOOLS = ['edit', 'write', 'apply_patch'] and aliases all three to the same permission name 'edit' during rule evaluation. A user writing 'edit: deny' blocks all three uniformly.

Dirge keeps them as separate permission tools (one rule namespace each). A user writing 'permission: { edit: { **: deny } }' expecting to lock down all edits still has write and apply_patch silently going through (post-M4 they Ask, but a follow-up 'write: allow' from the user would unintentionally re-open the gate).

Port: in enforce() at src/agent/tools/mod.rs, when the tool name is 'write' or 'apply_patch', ALSO consult the 'edit' rules. Take the more restrictive result of the two checks (any deny wins, any explicit ask beats allow). Document the aliasing in PermissionConfig docs.

title: Perm M4: flip unmatched-tool default from Allow to Ask
description:
Today src/permission/checker.rs:103 has default_action = Action::Allow. Anything not explicitly configured falls through to Allow without prompting — a security gap especially for write/edit/apply_patch which currently have NO default rules installed at all (mismatch with bash which has default_bash_rules, mcp_tool which defaults Ask since 27bd70a).

Flip default to Ask. Add builtin-allow list for safe read-only tools (port maki's BUILTIN_ALLOW_RULES at permissions.rs:16-24, adapted for dirge's tool set: read/glob/grep/list_dir/list_symbols/find_definition/find_callers/find_callees/get_symbol_body/repo_overview).

write/edit/apply_patch/bash/webfetch/websearch/task/skill/memory all become Ask by default unless explicitly allowlisted. Document the migration in README + CHANGELOG. Users with existing configs unaffected — only the no-config baseline changes.

Add --yolo CLI flag (currently only via config). Maps to set the global allow_all atomic (port maki's PermissionManager::toggle_yolo at permissions.rs:219-222).

Depends on: dirge-{M2}, dirge-{M3}

title: Perm M1: single chokepoint (port maki's enforce shape)
description:
Refactor dirge's 3 permission entry points (check_perm, check_perm_path, check_perm_path_resolve in src/agent/tools/mod.rs) into a single chokepoint patterned after maki's PermissionManager::enforce (maki-agent/src/permissions.rs:283). One function, takes (tool, scope), routes internally based on tool category. No behavior change for users — pure refactor with all existing callers updated. Behind-the-scenes the new fn calls the same PermissionChecker logic.

Ref: maki-agent/src/permissions.rs:283-350 — port the signature shape (async, returns Result<(), PermissionError>, takes &EventSender + user_response_rx + cancel for UI integration). Adapt to dirge's existing AskSender + PermCheck.

title: Perm M2: unified rule schema (port maki's TOML shape via JSON)
description:
Replace dirge's per-tool field PermissionConfig (separate Option<ToolPerm> per built-in tool) with maki's uniform shape: { tool_name: { allow: [patterns], deny: [patterns] } }. Single PermissionRule struct (maki-config/src/lib.rs:268-273). Keep existing JSON config (dirge uses JSON not TOML), but flatten the schema. Add a dual-read path so old config.json files (existing user configs in the wild) still parse — log a deprecation warning and auto-migrate on save. Migration test required.

Depends on: dirge-{M1}

title: UI: per-tool-call-id chamber state (parallel tool execution)
description: The UI tracks chamber state via a single bool tool_chamber_open + Option<String> last_tool_name. Under parallel tool execution (the default per agent_loop/types.rs:402), multiple ToolExecutionStart events fire before any ToolExecutionEnd. Subsequent ToolCalls close prior chambers prematurely (now via passive close after 7403792, previously with false 'denied' wording). ToolResults that arrive after a newer ToolCall's chamber opens land as out-of-place '↳ trailers' below the wrong chamber. Fix: chamber state keyed by tool_call_id (HashMap), each in-flight tool gets its own chamber frame, the abort/passive close functions take an id.

title: H-batch1-2: history up/down byte-offset panic risk on multi-byte input
description: ui/input.rs:988-1023 — col = self.cursor - line_start is byte distance; self.cursor = (pos + col).min(target_line_end) can land mid-codepoint when previous line has multi-byte chars. Subsequent replace_range/slice panics. Convert column to char index then back to byte offset on target line.

title: H-batch1-1: bash inherits all env vars including API keys
description: tools/bash.rs:208-320 no .env_clear(). Sensitive vars (OPENROUTER_API_KEY, EXA_API_KEY, PARALLEL_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, etc.) flow to every bash child. Use .env_clear() + curated allowlist (PATH, HOME, USER, LANG, TERM, etc.). See pi bash-executor.ts.

title: C8: /cd, /worktree, /wt-exit, /prompt, /regen-prompts silently drop LSP
description: ui/slash.rs:1290, 1300, 1763 + analogues in mod.rs — every build_agent call after these commands passes None for lsp_manager. User silently loses LSP for the rest of the session after first /cd. Re-thread lsp_manager (and re-root for /cd/worktree). opencode cd handler re-roots LSP.

title: C7: serialize_conversation drops tool_calls entirely
description: provider.rs:418-429 emits only [role]: content; msg.tool_calls (args, results, errors) invisible to summarizer. Tool-heavy sessions lose bulk of state post-compact. Include structured tool I/O like pi's compaction prompt.

title: C6: compaction summary truncates input to 6000 chars
description: provider.rs:312-320 cuts the prefix to first 6KB then appends '... [truncated]'. A 300K-token session is summarized from ~1500 tokens of content. Feed the full prefix (opencode session/compaction.ts:136-294) or chunk-summarize (pi compaction/). Pair fix with C7.

title: C5: ! cmd injects shell output into LLM context unfenced
C4: bash redirection target not permission-checked
description: tools/bash.rs:322-339 and semantic-bash never inspect redirect targets. echo pwned > /etc/something matches the safe echo ** rule and writes to /etc/something. Extract redirect/path args per command and route through path gate. See opencode shell.ts:191-208 pathArgs.

title: C3: compound_statement bypass in semantic-bash splitter
description: semantic/adapters/bash.rs:162-175 treats compound_statement/if_statement/while_statement/for_statement/case_statement/function_definition as opaque single segments. { rm -rf /tmp/foo; } matches no per-command rule and falls through to Allow. is_complex (line 127) doesn't flag these either. Mirror opencode shell.ts:127 using descendantsOfType('command') — recurse into every command descendant.

title: C2: webfetch SSRF via HTTP redirects
description: tools/webfetch.rs:270-273 builds reqwest client without redirect::Policy::none() or custom policy. validate_url_host_safety runs once on initial URL; 30x to RFC1918/169.254 is silently followed. Install custom redirect::Policy::custom re-running validate_url_host_safety on each Attempt::url().

title: C1: apply_patch uses unresolved path after perm check (symlink-swap risk)
description: tools/apply_patch.rs:251 calls check_perm_path (not check_perm_path_resolve), then apply_create/update/delete/rename operate on raw user-supplied path. Other tools (read.rs:161, write.rs:88, edit.rs:133) route through resolved_path. H12 fix didn't propagate. Thread check_perm_path_resolve per op and pass resolved string into apply_*.

title: H1: propagate AbortSignal into JanetLoopTool::execute
description: JanetLoopTool::execute takes _signal and drops it. Plugin tool handlers run unboundedly inside spawn_blocking holding the PM mutex; the user's Ctrl+C/Esc has no effect until the handler returns. Phase-6 wired AbortSignal into native tools; plugin tools must too.\n\nFix: race the spawn_blocking join against wait_for_cancel(signal) (same pattern as the phase-6 wrapper in builder.rs). On cancel return Err("cancelled"). Document that Janet is single-threaded so handlers can't be preempted — only joined. Plugin authors should keep work bounded.

title: C1/C2: fix customType wrapper + add e2e bridge test for renderer dispatch
description: Phase 9d renderer lookup uses wrong JSON nesting level — payload.get('type') against the {role,content} wrapper always returns None, so registered renderers never fire end-to-end. Smoke test calls invoke_message_renderer directly with a hand-built payload so it passes a broken path.\n\nFix: extend harness/add-custom-message to (customType content &opt display); wrap as {role:'custom', customType, content, display:true} matching pi's CustomMessage shape (messages.ts:48). UI consumer reads customType at top level. Add bridge/UI-equivalent test that walks the full LoopMessage::Custom -> AgentEvent::CustomMessage -> renderer-resolve path so future drift is caught.

title: Path traversal via .. survives lexical-fallback when both canonicalize calls fail
description: permission/checker.rs:495-513 — when canonicalize(joined) AND canonicalize(parent) both fail (nonexistent subdir + ..), falls through to lexical joined.to_string_lossy(). Path::starts_with matches components, so /cwd/nonexistent/../../etc/passwd classifies as internal. Need lexical .. normalization in fallback.

title: Provider SSE stream has no per-chunk timeout
description: agent/runner.rs:259 stream.next().await with no tokio::time::timeout — stalled provider hangs agent forever. opencode chunk-timeout pattern: per-chunk read deadline.

title: ANSI injection in permission ALERT prompt
description: ask_req.tool / ask_req.input rendered un-sanitized at mod.rs:2584-2585. Reopen path already sanitizes — asymmetric. Sec impl: ANSI at the permission-decision moment.

title: Chat history ignores 120-col content_width cap
description: max_line_width and wrap_line use raw content_cols, so on wide terminals scrollback overflows the centered band into divider/panel margin.

title: R1: fix 3 critical plugin bugs (FFI panic, dialog deadlock, init hang)
description: From the plugin subsystem audit: (1) wrap JanetCFunctions in catch_unwind so Rust panics don't unwind across the C-FFI boundary into Janet; (2) cancel send_dialog's reply_rx.recv() on worker shutdown so the worker thread doesn't block forever when the UI exits mid-dialog; (3) add timeout to the init handshake so a worker panic before init_tx.send() doesn't hang the main thread. Also: (4) bounds-assert wrap_string's i32 cast for the unlikely >2GB case, (5) make take_string_slot atomic to close the race window, (6) don't eat unrelated user events in the dialog arm.

title: Usage-driven memory curation + cadence fixes
description: Round 4 of SQLite memory plan. expand/use events bump use_count/last_used_at; salience reinforcement on use, decay on disuse; eviction = demotion (hot -> breadcrumb -> tombstoned) ordered by salience x recency. Curator consumes usage data; first curator run gated on session count not 7 days; <30-day contradictions eligible under budget pressure.

title: Memory lifecycle: tombstone instead of delete, id-addressed CRUD
description: Round 2 of SQLite memory plan. Evict/remove set status='tombstoned' instead of deleting rows; view and prompt injection exclude tombstoned; memory tool accepts id addressing (substring still works); curator gains archive/restore. Depends on SQLite backend swap.
close_reason: duplicate of dirge-8h22 (Phase 3: supersession/tombstone); that issue covers Round 2, to be implemented on the SQLite backend

title: Memory breadcrumb tier: inline index + expand/search actions
description: Round 3 of SQLite memory plan. Tiered prompt injection: hot tier verbatim, rest as one-line [id] (kind) preview breadcrumbs. memory tool gains expand (by id) and search (FTS over memories_fts) actions. Breadcrumb tier budget raised 10-50x. Index stays inline in system prompt; expansion is one tool call (weak-model-safe per arXiv:2605.15184).

title: Phase 3: Non-destructive supersession + bi-temporal time for memory
description:
Add UMP-compatible non-destructive supersession and bi-temporal time model to memory entries.

## Current State
Memory replace() mutates entries in-place via substring match. No history, no supersession chain, no valid time tracking. Remove() deletes entirely.

## Goal
- Add supersedes/superseded_by fields to track replacement chains
- Add time model with valid_from/valid_to (bi-temporal)  
- Replace operations create successors rather than mutating
- Superseded entries are tombstoned, not deleted
- Remove() tombstones by default (optional hard erase)
- Backward-compatible: existing entries get valid_from=now, valid_to=null

## Files
- src/extras/memory_store.rs — add time/supersession fields, update replace/remove logic
- src/extras/memory_provider.rs — update trait signatures if needed
- src/agent/tools/memory.rs — update tool definition for new semantics
- src/extras/memory_usage.rs — track tombstoned entries
notes: Implement against the SQLite memories table (dirge-18ks), not memory_store.rs markdown files. superseded_by column already planned in the v7 schema.
close_reason: tombstone-on-remove/evict + restore + id addressing implemented on sqlite-memory-store branch; replace deliberately stays update-in-place (lineage preserved on the row), bi-temporal valid_from/valid_to dropped as unneeded — reopen if true supersession history becomes a requirement

title: Phase 2: Injection-resistant rehydration framing for memory
description:
Add UMP-style injection-resistant framing wrapper when memory is injected into the system prompt.

## Current State
Memory entries are injected as raw text blocks wrapped in  XML tags. No explicit untrusted-data framing, no sanitization against fence-breaking.

## Goal
- Wrap injected memory in an explicit untrusted-data frame with structural fencing
- Sanitize entry content to prevent fence-breaking (strip forged frame tags, collapse newlines)
- Add a preamble directive that recalled memory is reference data, not instructions

## Files
- src/extras/memory_store.rs — update format_for_system_prompt to use framed output

title: Phase 1: Record identity + kinds + lifecycle for memory entries
description:
Add UMP-compatible record identity, memory kinds, and lifecycle fields to the memory system.

## Current State
Memory entries are plain strings separated by § delimiters in MEMORY.md / PITFALLS.md. No per-entry IDs, no kind classification, no lifecycle tracking.

## Goal
- Add stable, unique record IDs (URN format) per entry
- Add 5 memory kinds: semantic, episodic, procedural, working, identity
- Add lifecycle fields: confidence (0-1), salience (0-1), status (active/candidate/tombstoned)
- Backward-compatible: existing plain-text entries get auto-assigned IDs and default kind=procedural

## Files
- src/extras/memory_store.rs — add id/kind/lifecycle to MemoryStore entry model, update format_for_system_prompt
- src/extras/memory_provider.rs — add kind param to add(), update trait if needed
- src/agent/tools/memory.rs — add kind enum to tool definition, update Args

title: Unified input mechanism: InputMode state machine, dissolve nested modal loops (#387 follow-up)
description: Follow-up to the rendering refactor. User input is handled in 7 scattered places: the main user_rx arm (compose) + 6 nested blocking loops (permission, question options, question custom-input, dialog confirm, dialog select, plan accept/reject) + rewind picker. The nested blocking loops are the root cause of the questionnaire-freeze class of bug and the input-priority concern. Introduce InputMode enum on UiState + one dispatch_input router; dissolve each nested loop into a mode state driven by the central loop; make select! biased so user events take priority. Branch: refactor/unified-rendering-387.
close_reason: Dissolved all nested modal blocking loops into a unified InputMode state machine on UiState + dispatch_modal! router in the single biased user_rx arm. Converted plan-switch, question (incl. custom-text), dialog confirm/select, and permission; rewind picker was already event-driven. Modal-triggering arms gated on !is_modal() so siblings can't clobber an in-flight reply channel. Full --all-features suite green (2603). Commits on refactor/unified-rendering-387; needs interactive smoke-test of each modal before merge.

title: Unified state-driven rendering: single paint per event + UiState struct (#387)
description: GitHub issue #387. Phase 1: centralize all rendering into a single paint per event — add a needs_paint dirty flag + flush() to Renderer, make mutators set the flag instead of inline tui_redraw, build the StatusLine once per event at the loop bottom, collapse ~85 inline render_viewport/draw_bottom/StatusLine call sites. Phase 2: decouple rendering from state by moving the ~37 event-loop state locals into a UiState data structure that RunCtx + the status line read from. Branch: refactor/unified-rendering-387.
notes: DONE on branch refactor/unified-rendering-387 (5 commits). Phase1: UiState data model + Renderer single-paint mechanism (needs_paint/flush/set_bottom). Phase2: migrated 34 event-loop locals into UiState; render_frame! is the single per-event paint effect (builds StatusLine once, dirty-on-change coalescing preserved); collapsed ~85 inline draw_bottom/render_viewport sites (StatusLine::render now appears once in mod.rs, net -300+ lines). Full --all-features suite green. User verified live behavior. Ready for PR.

title: transform-context/on-compact block a tokio worker (no spawn_blocking); message-end skipped in headless
description: agent_loop/plugin_hooks.rs:451-468,498-535: transform_context_from_plugin_manager, on_before, on_compact call mgr.dispatch() directly in the async block while holding the PluginManager std-Mutex, blocking the OS thread up to INTERACTIVE_EVAL_TIMEOUT (30s) per LLM call — unlike tool hooks which use spawn_blocking + timeout (LOOP-8). Separately, message-end / harness/rewrite-message is dispatched only on the interactive done.rs path (:180), never on the headless provider/run.rs path — a silent no-op under --print/--loop/ACP. Fix: wrap these dispatches in spawn_blocking; fire message-end on the headless path too (or document TUI-only).
close_reason: transform-context/on-before-compact/on-compact now run via spawn_blocking + 10s timeout (no tokio-worker stall, PM mutex not held across a slow hook). message-end now also dispatched on the headless apply_response_hooks path with the rewrite stored. Tests added.

title: Phased /plan forks receive the full session transcript, not just explore findings
description: cmd_plan.rs:147-162 + spawn.rs:353 + review.rs:687: both explore and plan forks are passed transcript=build_transcript(session) (entire session history) embedded as <session_transcript>; the plan fork additionally gets findings. The in-code comment 'the ONLY thing carried over is the findings report — a true context reset between phases' is inaccurate: phases are isolated from each other's intermediate tool chatter but NOT from prior session history. Fix: either restrict the plan fork to findings-only as documented, or correct the comment/docs.
close_reason: Plan fork now spawned with an empty transcript (findings-only via plan_prompt) instead of the full session transcript — matches the documented 'true context reset between phases'. Explore fork keeps the transcript as entry context. Plan suite green.

title: context-depth tracker counts per tool-call, not per turn, contradicting its docs/reminder
description: context_depth.rs docstring, Inner.consecutive, and the emitted reminder all say 'consecutive turns', but record_tool_call is invoked once per tool call (tools.rs:170 sequential, :879 parallel -> :443). Test touches_same_files_increments_consecutive confirms 3 calls => consecutive=3. A single turn with 5 edits to one file bumps consecutive by 5, so the reminder fires far earlier than 'N turns' implies and reports an inflated count. Fix: count per turn, or correct the wording/threshold.
close_reason: Wording corrected across module docstring, Inner field comments, the emitted reminder, features.md, and config.md to say 'consecutive tool calls' (the real per-call unit) instead of 'turns'. Behavior unchanged; test updated.

title: DAP has no session-end teardown; relies on Drop of a static that never runs
description: dap/session.rs:29: DAP_MANAGER is a static Option<Arc<DapSessionManager>>. Debuggee/adapter teardown depends on DapProcessGuard::drop -> kill(-pgid, SIGKILL), but a static is never dropped on normal process exit, and there is no exit hook calling terminate_active/disconnect (contrast LSP's close_all_files at main.rs:1273). Within a session terminate_active runs before each new launch, but on dirge exit a setsid-isolated adapter+debuggee can be orphaned. Fix: add a session-end/atexit hook that terminates the active DAP session.
close_reason: Added dap::session::shutdown_active_session() (terminates the active session via terminate_active) and called it on the interactive exit path beside the LSP close_all_files step, so a DAP adapter+debuggee isn't orphaned when the static manager is never Dropped.

title: /panel auto real threshold is ~152 cols, not the documented 100; PANEL_AUTO_MIN_COLS is dead
description: renderer.rs:1103-1110: side_panel_visible for Auto requires cols>=100 AND content_indent()>=15. content_indent (=:1163) only reaches 15 once cols>=152 (content_width capped at 120). So between 100 and ~151 cols /panel auto (and /panel on) show nothing, and PANEL_AUTO_MIN_COLS=100 (:108) is effectively dead — the indent>=15 gate always binds. README:126 says '>=100 cols'. Fix: reconcile the constant + docs with the real threshold.
close_reason: PANEL_AUTO_MIN_COLS set to 152 (the real cols at which content_indent reaches 15); README/config/features docs corrected from >=100 to >=152. Test pins the constant to the gutter math.

title: --no-color is bypassed by hardcoded diff +/- background ANSI escapes
description: ui/run_handlers/tool_result.rs:310-315 -> box_render.rs:283-290 bake \x1b[48;5;22m (green) / \x1b[48;5;52m (red) directly into diff rows. write_line stores the string verbatim; theme::no_color() (theme.rs:481) only collapses colors routed through theme::*() accessors. Under --no-color, diff lines still paint green/red backgrounds — the one stray-escape bypass of the single-chokepoint design. Fix: source the diff backgrounds from the theme chokepoint.
close_reason: row_with_bg now sources its +/- background escapes via diff_bg_escapes(bg_idx, theme::no_color()) — empty under --no-color. Pure helper tested.

title: apply_summary/compute_compress_window can orphan a tool_use/tool_result pair (latent 400)
description: compression.rs:691-742: the compaction window is message-count based (end = n - protect_tail). assistant tool_calls and each tool_result are separate messages (run.rs:1071,1104,1117). The tail/head boundary can leave an orphaned tool_result (or retain an assistant tool_call whose result was folded), producing an unbalanced history that 400s Anthropic/OpenAI. heal/fix_tool_call_pairing runs ONLY on session load (integration.rs:628), never after a fold. The handle_compress path guards this via align_cut_to_user_boundary (mod.rs:91) — the two compaction impls diverged. LATENT today only because summarize_fn is unwired; becomes live HIGH if that is wired. Fix: snap the window to tool-pair/user boundaries or re-run fix_tool_call_pairing after every fold.
close_reason: compute_compress_window now snaps both cuts to user-message boundaries (head forward, tail backward) so the fold can't orphan a tool_use/tool_result pair. Test: compute_window_snaps_off_tool_pairs.

title: prune_tool_outputs silently no-ops on production block-array tool results
description: compression.rs:419 reads msg content via as_str(); production tool results are block arrays (message.rs:289-298, content = Vec<ContentBlock>). Array content => None => never pruned. So the only compaction that runs in the live loop does nothing to real tool results; headroom is held solely by cap_oversized_tool_results (run.rs:912). The estimator and the cap were widened for blocks (dirge-el3n); this pass was not. Fix: handle block-array content in prune_tool_outputs. (Closely tied to the summarize_fn-dead issue.)
close_reason: prune_tool_outputs now reads text from both scalar-string and block-array content and preserves the array shape on rewrite; the in-loop prune pass (run.rs:378) is no longer a no-op on real tool results. Tests: prune_handles_block_array_content, prune_leaves_small_block_array_untouched.

title: allow_tools does not restrict MCP or plugin tools (reads as a whitelist, only caps built-ins)
description: context/agent_defs.rs:72-83: ToolPolicy::Allow is realized as 'deny every built-in not in the allow-list' over BUILTIN_TOOL_NAMES. MCP/plugin tools are not enumerable there, so a profile allow_tools: [read, grep] leaves every MCP/plugin tool fully callable. Users reasonably read allow_tools as a hard whitelist. Fix: extend allow_tools to deny non-listed MCP/plugin tools (e.g. deny mcp_tool umbrella + unlisted plugin tools), or document the limitation prominently.
close_reason: Added plugin_tool to BUILTIN_TOOL_NAMES so ToolPolicy::Allow->deny denies the plugin umbrella (mcp_tool was already covered). allow_tools is now a genuine cap over built-ins + MCP + plugin; listing an umbrella re-allows that class. Doc corrected, test added.

title: edit_minified is invisible to the verifier gate and misclassified by the storm breaker
description: verifier.rs:76 hardcodes match tool_name { write|edit|apply_patch }, so an agent editing exclusively via edit_minified (a registered built-in, tools/mod.rs:97) never sets edited_code and the 'verify before done' gate stays silent on unverified code changes. Separately, permission/engine/build.rs:42-62 maps edit/apply_patch/write to Operation::Edit but edit_minified falls through to Operation::Other, so storm (storm.rs:209) does not treat it as mutating (a verify-read after a minified edit can be miscounted). MITIGATED: edit_minified presents as 'edit' to enforce, so plan-mode permission denies still cover it. Fix: derive verifier + storm classification from tool_operation and add edit_minified to the Edit mapping.
close_reason: Added edit_minified->Edit and read_minified->Read to tool_operation (fixes storm default_mutating/default_exempt) and edit_minified to the verifier gate's edit arm. Tests added.

title: /agent off reverts prompt + tools but not the model (model swap leaks)
description: cmd_model.rs:389-408: /agent off clears current_agent/current_prompt/current_prompt_name/current_prompt_deny_tools and rebuilds, but never restores session.model to its pre-profile value. After /agent architect (model->opus) then /agent off, the session stays on opus. Also profile->profile switches where the new profile has no model leave the prior profile's model active. Documented as a deliberate asymmetry but a sharp edge. Fix: snapshot and restore the model on 'off'.
close_reason: Unified composition layer (ContextFiles::recompute_composition): /prompt and /agent are independent layers folded into the effective fields. Denies UNION (no clobber); /agent off restores prompt layer + pre-agent model (model_before_agent). All set-sites routed through layer setters. 6 composition unit tests.

title: summarization_provider and subagent_provider are dead config (advertised as live)
description: Both role keys (config/mod.rs:319-321,449-456) are resolved only for the /agents status display (cmd_misc.rs:72,85) and tests — never consulted at runtime. Compaction always uses session.model (dispatch.rs:115); subagents use the profile/main model (task.rs:440, main.rs:816-832). docs/agents.md:139-140 and the /agents view claim they route work elsewhere. Confirmed by provider + session reviews. Fix: either consume them or stop advertising them.
close_reason: summarization_provider + subagent_provider now consumed at runtime; docs corrected; test added.

title: Route task/subagent + /plan phases to named agent profiles (dirge-ykeu Phase 4)
description: Phases 1-3 (define profiles, /agent switch of the main loop's model+prompt+tools, unified /agents view) are merged. Phase 4: let the task tool spawn a subagent using a named agent profile (its model + system prompt + tool policy) via an optional 'agent' Args field, and let /plan phases bind to profiles. Requires threading the AgentRegistry into TaskTool (built in build_loop_tools) and the plan runtime. Also: cross-provider client switching for /agent (currently same-client model string only). Separate PR — touches the subagent/plan execution path.
notes: task(agent=<name>) subagent routing implemented in PR #386 (branch feat/agent-subagent-routing): per-subagent model + system prompt via a startup-resolved process-global routing table. REMAINING in this issue: (1) route /plan phases to named profiles, (2) cross-provider client switching for /agent (currently same-client model string), (3) apply profile reasoning/temperature on the subagent path.

title: Config-driven, opt-in multi-agent mechanism (unify role-routing + critic + user agents)
description: dirge already routes ROLES to different models via config (provider / review_provider[critic] / escalation_provider / compact_model / subagent). Forge generalizes this: user-defined agents in .forge/agents/*.md (id, system prompt, tools, model, reasoning), lazy-loaded into a registry, each overriding model/provider, layered CWD->global->builtin; agents become /agent-<name> commands. DESIGN GOAL for dirge: a UNIFIED, OPT-IN, config-driven agent mechanism where the existing roles (critic/escalation/summarizer/subagent) are special built-in agents and users can define custom ones (name + prompt + tools + model + reasoning) in config.json or .dirge/agents/. Opt-in: absent config = today's single-agent behavior unchanged. Brings: /agent-<name> commands, per-agent model override. Reuse: adapt Forge's agent_definition.rs schema + agent_registry.rs (forge_repo/forge_services). HIGH value, needs a design spike first. Source: ~/src/forgecode.
design:
UNIFIED AGENT MECHANISM (opt-in, config-driven). Generalize the existing ConfigRole (Default/Review/Escalation/Summarization/Subagent/Critic/Approval — each a config key routing to a providers-alias model via resolve_role) into a named AGENT = { prompt, model, tools, reasoning, temperature }. Built-in roles become reserved built-in agents (today model-only); users can define custom agents and override built-ins (e.g. give the critic a custom prompt). role->model becomes the model slice of agent->{model,prompt,tools}.

CONFIG SOURCE (decided: BOTH, layered): (a) .dirge/agents/<name>.md — YAML frontmatter + prompt body, SAME shape as dirge skills (reuse the skill frontmatter parser), project dir + global ~/.config/dirge/agents/; (b) config.json agents:{ name: { model, prompt, tools:{allow|deny}, reasoning, temperature } } for inline one-offs. PRECEDENCE: project file > global file > config.json > built-in. No agents configured => today's single-agent behavior unchanged (fully additive/opt-in). The existing *_provider keys stay as backward-compat sugar that sets a built-in agent's model.

AGENT FIELDS: name; prompt (inline | @file | named dirge prompt | none->default); model (Option<providers alias>; None->default, reuses resolve_role plumbing); tools (all | {allow:[..]} | {deny:[..]} — reuse prompt deny_tools enforcement at the permission layer); reasoning effort/budget; temperature.

INVOCATION: /agent interactive picker + dynamic /agent-<name> tab-completable commands that switch the active agent (prompt/model/tools) for the session (reuse Forge's sanitize+register from plans/2025-09-13-dynamic-agent-slash-commands-v3.md). Later: /plan phases + the task subagent can target a named agent.

REUSE (port, don't rewrite): Forge crates/forge_repo/src/agent_definition.rs (schema/frontmatter) + crates/forge_services/src/agent_registry.rs (lazy-load + layering + precedence) + plans/2025-09-08-agent-loader-cwd-extension-v1.md (CWD->global->builtin precedence) + plans/2025-09-13-dynamic-agent-slash-commands-v3.md. dirge side: reuse src/skill.rs frontmatter parser, config resolve_role plumbing, prompt deny_tools enforcement.

PHASED DELIVERY: (1) AgentDefinition + loader (both sources) + registry with precedence + guardrail test — LOAD ONLY, no behavior change. (2) /agent picker + /agent-<name> commands switch active agent. (3) fold built-in roles (critic/review/escalation/summarizer/subagent/approval) into the registry; *_provider keys become sugar. (4) optional — route /plan phases + task subagents to named agents.
notes: Phases 1-3 implemented in PR #385 (branch feat/agent-profiles), all CI green: AgentDefinition/AgentRegistry loader (3 layered sources), /agent <name> switching (model+prompt+tools, same-client), /agents unified view incl. built-in role routing. Built-in critic/role routing untouched (opt-in preserved). Phase 4 (route task/subagent + /plan to named profiles; cross-provider /agent client switch) filed as separate follow-up.

title: Add non-interactive 'resume session by id' CLI flag
description: dirge has -p/--print (one-shot) and -r/--resume (INTERACTIVE session browse). A non-interactive 'resume a specific session by id' flag (e.g. --session <id> / --continue) is missing. Prereq for the shell-plugin (:prefix) mode: that plugin keeps all : commands in one conversation by passing a stable id to a headless dirge -p run. Add the flag + wire it to load the named session before the print/loop run. From Forge codebase review (forge --conversation-id).

title: Tool-description quality audit (adopt Forge's standard)
description: Forge's docs/tool-guidelines.md codifies tool-description quality (what / when / when-NOT / param meanings / limitations; <=1024 chars, enforced by tests). dirge's descriptions are strong on some tools (with_contract_hint) but variable elsewhere. Audit every tool's description() and upgrade weak ones to the standard. Docs-only; improves model tool-selection accuracy. Low effort. Source: ~/src/forgecode/docs/tool-guidelines.md. From Forge codebase review.
close_reason: Merged in #381 — descriptions audited + 1024-char guardrail test

title: Internal nudges (todo/verifier/critic) misattributed to <you> in the UI
description: The todo-completion nudge ('You still have N unfinished todos...'), and likely the verifier/critic follow-ups, are injected as LoopMessage::User so the model responds to them — but the UI renders them with the <you> handle, attributing system-generated steering to the user. They should display with a distinct system/critic attribution (or be hidden from the visible transcript), not as user input. Relates to dirge-vcsn (poll_finalization_follow_up) which builds these as User messages.
close_reason: Merged in #375 — nudges attributed to <critic> via finalization_nudge_body

title: /compact (explicit) should force compaction, not be gated by threshold
description: When the loop auto-compacts it correctly checks context is over threshold (should_compress / decide_after_usage). But an explicit user /compact command is currently treated like a suggestion — it appears to be gated by the same threshold check, so /compact below the threshold is a no-op. Explicit /compact must be definitive: force the compaction pass regardless of current context fraction. Separate the 'auto' path (threshold-gated) from the 'user-commanded' path (forced).

title: Unify the mid-loop interjection layer (5+ inject sites)
description: Steering, context_depth, verifier, critic, todo-nudge, and reflexion each have their own trigger + channel + integration point, firing at three different places in run.rs (inner poll ~:69, finalize gates :1327-1377, storm guard :1002) with ad-hoc precedence. Notably verifier silently blocks critic via 'if follow_up.is_empty()' so the critic verdict never surfaces on a turn that also has a red build, and MID_TURN_STEER_WRAPPER is applied at two independent sites. Design + build one interjection abstraction with explicit priority and a single wrap-once point. MED — top consolidation target for a smoother loop.
close_reason: Merged in #366 — poll_finalization_follow_up single precedence authority

title: Single context-budget authority (estimate+thresholds+action)
description: Three modules independently decide 'context is filling': compression.rs (pre-send per-result caps + should_compress), context_manager.rs (post-response fold/exit tiers), context_depth.rs (ambient reminder). Pre-send uses a chars/4 ESTIMATE while post-response uses the API's ACTUAL prompt_tokens — two disagreeing numbers — plus a snip-feedback override living only in run.rs that's invisible to the decision engine. Consolidate into one ContextBudget owning the estimate, all tier thresholds, and the chosen action. MED.
close_reason: Merged in #367 — canonical budget-ladder doc; concrete dup already resolved (single estimator; 75% in #365); decision/mechanism split preserved

title: Extract built-in tool collision filter (written 3×)
description: The same BUILTIN_TOOL_NAMES.contains(name){warn;skip} block is copy-pasted at builder/loop_tools.rs:41, :482, :520 (MCP / plugin / semantic registration). Extract one generic filter_by_builtin_names() and call once per source. MED.

title: Round A: find_callees_in_range + ext_of + spawn helpers + short_id
description: #2 hoist the byte-identical parser+query+sort/dedup envelope of find_callees_in_range across 10 semantic adapters into common::run_callee_query(lang,query,src,range) (~280 LOC). #5 tools::ext_of(path) for the 8 .extension().and_then(...).unwrap_or('') sites. #9 spawn.rs private tool_defs_for()/model_name_opt() helpers. #10 short_id(id) for the 9 id.chars().take(8) sites (2 already-dup local fns). All S-effort, near-zero risk, test-pinned.

title: Codebase-wide duplication consolidation (post-vix audit)
description: wavescope overview + 4 parallel dup-hunt subagents surfaced genuine consolidation candidates across semantic adapters, UI text processing, tools, and provider. Execute in rounds A-D, each its own PR. Several suspected dups were correctly dismissed (fork-spawn family already consolidated; only one fenced-block parser; rig::Tool boilerplate not abstractable).

Several proposed merges honestly declined after feasibility review (ext_of overcount, transcript unification, head/tail truncators).

title: Extract 'launch streamed run' helper (set agent_rx/abort/interject/cancel + is_running)
description: The spawn_runner + set-four-mutables + is_running=true block is copy-pasted 5x (ui/mod.rs kickoff + loop-iter; done.rs followup/loopiter/retry). Most bug-prone duplication — forget one mutable => leaked task. Factor into one helper.

description: AbortRunnerOnDrop duplicated verbatim in review.rs + phased_orchestrator.rs. Event-drain loop hand-rolled 5x (4 in review.rs + collect_runner_text) + 3x tool-action fold. Hoist guard to runner.rs; add generic drain_runner helper + summarize_actions. Safety-critical (abort guard prevents orphaned runners).

Delete them + the allow; keep shared primitives (next_review_step, prompts, parse_review_verdict).

description: After execute, fork a write-disabled reviewer (read+bash, NO write/edit) that independently runs the code, emits a fenced JSON verdict {verdict: DONE|NEEDS_FIX, missing:[...]} (asymmetric: ambiguous -> NEEDS_FIX). Parse it; on NEEDS_FIX feed the punch-list to a bounded implement-retry. Closes dirge-sff7. Builds on P3a.

Context handoff = prior phase output into next phase transcript. Hard context reset per phase (separate forked agents). Hooks into the runtime plan-mode path. Implements dirge-ww6k (forks) too.

Reuses the existing parameterized tool-allowlist machinery. Foundation for P3c/P3d.

Requires per-language formatters (rustfmt/black/gofmt/...) — vix runs configured formatters; dirge needs them or the file is left minified. Port vix vfs.go VfsEdit semantics incl. per-file mutex + read-gate.
